subject: Physics
title: Georg Ohm's Troubled Legacy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Georg Ohm's Troubled Legacy.

hook.text: Georg Ohm discovered the most used equation in electronics—and was forced to resign from his teaching position.

buildup.text: In 1827, Ohm published his law relating voltage, current, and resistance. It was elegant and simple.

discovery.text: The German establishment rejected his work. Critics called his mathematical approach inappropriate for physics.

twist.text: Ohm resigned in poverty and spent 6 years as a tutor before finally getting recognition from the Royal Society.

climax.text: Today, V = IR is taught in every physics class worldwide. The ohm is the universal unit of resistance.

punchline.text: Rejected. Resigned. Redeemed. The simplest equation in electronics.

quiz.question: Why was Georg Ohm's work initially rejected?
options: The German academic establishment considered his mathematical approach inappropriate
His law only worked for DC circuits
